notes: Round-3 labeling.py ablation #2: sub 33 + 2-param Platt scaling (a, b) fit on labeled[] in logit space by Newton-Raphson (5 steps) with ridge prior lambda=10 toward identity (a=1, b=0). a clamped to [0.5, 2.0]. Hypothesis: with K=25 labels and strong identity prior, (a, b) -> (1, 0) so the variant ties sub 33; provides a 2-dof upper bound on what label-driven recalibration can buy. No labeling.py (random platform fallback).
